private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $25M to date. The peer universe consists of two public comparables and eight private firms in the same sector and stage band, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for advancement from Seed to the next financing round, implying a $62M valuation. Peer-median analysis references four same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The peer cohort median is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The triangulated valuation range is $53M to $259M, with a median of $144M. The wide spread reflects material differences in peer maturity—three comparables are Series A while one is Seed—and heterogeneity in defense-tech valuations at this juncture. Key uncertainties include stage discount appropriate for Distributed Spectrum relative to Series A peers, technology differentiation versus the broader peer set, and near-term capital efficiency. The $62M–$225M band defined by the two methods provides a narrower working range pending additional operational or market data.
